description: A simple cropped screenshot of a Department of Labor news-release web page on a plain white background, with no added graphics, photos, seals, or meme styling. Black serif text reads a small 'News Release' kicker, then a large all-caps headline 'US DEPARTMENT OF LABOR LAUNCHES PROJECT FIREWALL TO PROTECT AMERICA'S HIGHLY SKILLED WORKFORCE,' followed by an italic subhead 'H-1B enforcement initiative aims to protect American jobs through federal agency partnerships.' A faint scrollbar is visible at right. The tweet frames it as 'Highly-skilled jobs should go to AMERICANS FIRST!' announcing Project Firewall to 'end H-1B abuse.' Because it is a plain document/webpage screenshot rather than a designed graphic, it is marked is_designed=false; the underlying message is the administration's H-1B-restriction, American-worker-first labor policy aimed at a general/policy audience.
notable_text: US DEPARTMENT OF LABOR LAUNCHES PROJECT FIREWALL TO PROTECT AMERICA'S HIGHLY SKILLED WORKFORCE; H-1B enforcement initiative
